TRIAL DATE IS FEBRUARY 23, 2015 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE TRIAL CASE MANAGEMENT ORDER DATED MAY 7, 2014 SHALL BE FOLLOWED BY THE PARTIES AS WELL AS ALL SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES AND CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES OF COURT RULE 5 ON FEBRUARY 23, 2015 AT 1:30P.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE/FURTHER DETAILS OF RULING.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: DENISE M. LOMBARDO OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5419

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Oct-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Plaintiff's motion for preference.

APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltf & moving party; Elisabeth A. Baum, Esq. of Walsworth Franklin Bevins & McCall, LLP 781-7072 for deft Thomas Dee Engineering Company; Linda L. Nusser, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 415/986-5900 for deft 3M Company; John Marston, Esq. of Jackson Jenkins Renstrom LLP 415/982-3600 for deft Dap, Inc.; Andre Hill, Esq. of Bassi, Edlin, Huie & Blum LLP 415/397-9006 for deft J.T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is February 23, 2015 at 11:15am in Department 503. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 19, 2015. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice dictated by the C.C.P., unless plaintiffs stipulate otherwise. Time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 5, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than December 12, 2014. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by February 13, 2015 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on February 19, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 6, 2015. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 20, 2015. No later than November 13, 2014 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than November 13, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on December 9, 2014 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on February 23, 2015 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by February 19, 2015 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Denise M. Lombardo of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5419 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)
